prevalence.value: ~5-15% (young children; varies by country and socioeconomic status)
prevalence.source: European child nutrition surveys and WHO anemia context
prevalence.trend: stable to slowly decreasing; persists in vulnerable groups

burden.dalyPerCapita: moderate in vulnerable groups; impacts development and growth
burden.mortalityRate: Indirect; generally low in high-resource settings
burden.source: IHME/GBD anemia context; WHO child nutrition

riskFactors: high cow’s milk intake in toddlers
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Hemoglobin below age thresholds with iron studies as needed; dietary and growth assessment; rule out chronic inflammation and malabsorption when indicated

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: variable; pediatric health visits may include hemoglobin checks for at-risk groups
publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Millions of children across the EU
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with supplementation and dietary intervention

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Primary care and pediatric services diagnose; lab access widely available
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Oral iron supplementation, diet counseling, food fortification strategies, address underlying causes
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Socioeconomic disparities, adherence issues due to GI side effects
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong primary care and lab access; effective low-cost treatment
